title: game studio goal_verify asset engine tests perf
purpose: F3.4 defines game studio goal verify asset engine tests perf for the agent-driven expert system. Organization — template-packs as whole-company ceiling.
scope: Owns `F3.4` only; siblings under `F3` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).

Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §8 — Branch F — Organization plane (template-packs = ceiling)](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#8-branch-f-organization-plane-template-packs-ceiling).
behavior_steps: When platform promotion lands playbooks, scripts, or pack fragments, reconcile-stale registers each reusable path in docs/manifest/staleness.json so downstream task cards and compose hits can mark dependencies stale.

On script or playbook change during pursuit, the staleness graph propagates to dependent task cards and [E2.2](E2.2-compose-query-catalog-list-components.md) compose results—workers reconcile before advancing implement steps.

Regeneration of [E1.1](E1.1-catalog-scripts-manifest-generated.md) and [E1.7](E1.7-catalog-platform-catalog-md-umbrella.md) reconciles graph entries with live catalog INDEX rows after platform drain completes.
Pack verify suites from [F1.8](F1.8-pack-verify-goal-verify-suites.md) may assert graph completeness at goal_verify—missing nodes fail closed rather than allowing pursuit on orphan artifact paths.

If a promoted artifact lacks a staleness node or graph drift leaves dependents unmarked, pursuit stops at H2 until reconcile-stale or [D4.5](D4.5-platform-work-catalog-regeneration.md) restores traceability.

Pass 3: cross-link related nodes in same branch index.

Validate `F3.4` against SEC-15 release checklist and parent index links.
Document `F3.4` in parent index with verify command and release tag.
Add checklist row in SEC-15 release doc for `F3.4`.
acceptance: [ ] Named script, skill, or test path exists or is listed in SEC-15 release row
narrative: F3.4 defines game studio goal verify asset engine tests perf for the agent-driven expert system. Organization — template-packs as whole-company ceiling. In practice, the system when platform promotion lands playbooks, scripts, or pack fragments, reconcile-stale registers each reusable path in docs/manifest/staleness.json so downstream task cards and compose hits can mark dependencies stale, then on script or playbook change during pursuit, the staleness graph propagates to dependent task cards and [e2.2](e2.2-compose-query-catalog-list-components.md) compose results—workers reconcile before advancing implement steps.
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent plane-f 2026-06-28 -->

Game studio goal_verify proves assets load in engine, automated tests pass, and performance budgets hold—beyond per-task pytest evidence. The suite aggregates tool-operator logs, engine test output, and perf counters into one `goal.verify_command` resolved at scope complete ([A2.4](A2.4-goal-scope-complete-run-goal-verify.md)).

Weak goal_verify (smoke import only) is an accepted risk documented in adversarial review—pack authors tighten suites over releases. Failure blocks H3 and packages H2 with evidence paths attached. Extends generic pack verify ([F1.8](F1.8-pack-verify-goal-verify-suites.md)) without replacing task-level verify-router discipline.
